title: Lobbyist / Corporate Affairs Manager
setting: Corporate affairs firm or in-house policy team, Sydney or Canberra
companySize: Small specialist advisory team or in-house corporate affairs function
description: Corporate affairs and lobbying work sits at the edge of government, business, and media. The day revolves around issue tracking, stakeholder mapping, meeting preparation, submissions, and message discipline.

schedule.activity: Monitor political and regulatory developments affecting clients or the business
Internal strategy meeting on active issues and stakeholder priorities
Prepare for a meeting with a ministerial adviser or department contact
Draft or revise a submission responding to a consultation process
Client or executive update on political risk and likely decision paths
Coordinate media and stakeholder alignment on a public position
Update the stakeholder map and note the next access point or pressure point

responsibilities: Build stakeholder maps and access plans
Advise executives or clients on political risk
challenges: Working through opaque timing and access constraints
Managing reputational risk for public-facing issues
rewards: Strong exposure to how external actors move government
Fast development in judgment, stakeholder handling, and writing
High visibility on live policy and regulatory change
careerPath: Move into government relations leadership or strategic advisory work
